tasks.title: Initialize React Native + Expo mobile app with development build
tasks.description: Set up mobile/ directory with Expo SDK 52+, expo-dev-client, Expo Router, TypeScript strict mode, and EAS build configuration
tasks.details:
1. Create mobile/ directory
2. Run `npx create-expo-app@latest mobile --template blank-typescript`
3. Configure app.json/app.config.js with development build settings
4. Install expo-dev-client, expo-router, react-native-maps
5. Set up TypeScript strict mode in tsconfig.json
6. Configure EAS build (eas.json) for development builds with Google Maps native support
7. Install expo-video for video playback
8. Create basic folder structure: app/, components/, lib/, types/, hooks/
9. Install and configure Supabase JS client v2+
10. Add environment variables setup (.env.example with SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY)
11. Generate TypeScript types from Supabase (already exists at supabase/types/database.types.ts, link to mobile/types/)
12. Test: Run dev build on iOS/Android simulator with hot reload working
tasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) npx expo start --dev-client launches successfully, (2) Hot reload works, (3) TypeScript compiles with strict mode, (4) Supabase client connects to remote project mvpvqmyhrrkwbnpctpuq, (5) EAS build configuration is valid

subtasks.title: Create mobile/ directory with Expo blank-typescript template
subtasks.description: Initialize the mobile app directory using create-expo-app with the blank-typescript template, then configure pnpm as package manager with node-linker=hoisted for Metro compatibility.
subtasks.details:
1. Create mobile/ directory at project root.
2. Run `npx create-expo-app@latest mobile --template blank-typescript` to scaffold Expo SDK 52+ with TypeScript.
3. Create `.npmrc` in mobile/ with `node-linker=hoisted` (required for Metro resolver with pnpm).
4. Run `pnpm install` to initialize dependencies.
5. Verify the scaffold works: `pnpm expo start` should launch without errors.
6. Clean up default App.tsx boilerplate to a minimal placeholder.

Files created:
- mobile/package.json
- mobile/tsconfig.json
- mobile/app.json
- mobile/.npmrc
- mobile/App.tsx

<info added on 2026-06-24T02:33:40.824Z>
CRITICAL ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED for EAS Gradle build failures: pnpm-workspace.yaml at project root (even containing only allowBuilds without listing packages) caused pnpm to treat entire repo as workspace and HOIST mobile/ dependencies to root-level node_modules. Result: @react-native/gradle-plugin and expo-modules-autolinking installed OUTSIDE mobile/node_modules. Since EAS uploads ONLY mobile/ directory, build received incomplete project and settings.gradle (running 'node --print require.resolve(...)') failed to locate plugins → 'Projects for build : have not been registered yet' error at 8s into Gradle.

ROOT FIX: Deleted pnpm-workspace.yaml from project root + reinstalled mobile/ as standalone. Verified: require.resolve now resolves to /mobile/node_modules/.pnpm/. mobile/ is now fully self-contained.

LESSON: mobile/ MUST be self-contained; NO pnpm-workspace.yaml must exist at root while mobile/ remains standalone. node-linker=hoisted in .npmrc is insufficient if parent workspace captures install. EAS limitation (uploads single directory) requires ALL dependencies including native modules be within mobile/node_modules.
</info added on 2026-06-24T02:33:40.824Z>

subtasks.title: Configure development build settings (app.json, eas.json) with Google Maps support
subtasks.description: Set up app.config.js/app.json for expo-dev-client development builds and configure EAS build (eas.json) with native Google Maps support for iOS and Android.
subtasks.details:
1. Install EAS CLI globally if needed, then run `pnpm add expo-dev-client`.
2. Convert app.json to app.config.js for dynamic config (env vars).
3. Configure development build settings:
   - slug: 'urbea'
   - ios.bundleIdentifier: 'com.urbea.app'
   - android.package: 'com.urbea.app'
   - ios.config.googleMapsApiKey from env
   - android.config.googleMaps.apiKey from env
4. Create eas.json with profiles:
   - development: developmentClient=true, distribution=internal
   - preview: distribution=internal
   - production: distribution=store
5. Add Google Maps API key placeholders to .env.example.
6. Run `eas build:configure` to validate configuration.

Files created/modified:
- mobile/app.config.js (or app.config.ts)
- mobile/eas.json
- mobile/.env.example (add GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY)

SQL: CREATE POLICY storage_videos_insert ON storage.objects FOR INSERT TO authenticated WITH CHECK (bucket_id = 'property-videos' AND (storage.foldername(name))[1] = auth.uid()::text AND private.current_user_role() = 'agent'). Add to the storage migration file.

subtasks.title: Create RLS SELECT policy for public video access
subtasks.description: Create storage RLS policy allowing public (anon) read access to videos only when the associated property has status='active'
subtasks.details: Create RLS policy on storage.objects for SELECT. Policy needs to join to properties table via path extraction. Path convention: {user_id}/{property_id}/{video_id}.mp4, so extract property_id from (storage.foldername(name))[2]. SQL: CREATE POLICY storage_videos_select ON storage.objects FOR SELECT TO anon, authenticated USING (bucket_id = 'property-videos' AND (EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.properties WHERE id = ((storage.foldername(name))[2])::uuid AND status = 'active' AND deleted_at IS NULL) OR auth.uid()::text = (storage.foldername(name))[1])). This allows: public read if property is active, or owner can always read their own uploads.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Unauthenticated user can read video for active property, (2) Unauthenticated user cannot read video for draft/paused/closed property, (3) Property owner can read their own videos regardless of property status, (4) Authenticated non-owner can read only active property videos.

subtasks.title: Implement invitation token validation logic (exists, not expired, uses < max)
subtasks.description: Add logic to validate the invitation code against agency_invitation_tokens table checking existence, expiration, revocation, and usage limits.
subtasks.details: Query agency_invitation_tokens using the SHA256 hash of the provided invitationCode (as per migration 0003: 'token almacena el HASH del token'). Use crypto.subtle.digest to hash the plain token before querying. Validation checks: (1) Token exists with matching hash, (2) revoked_at IS NULL, (3) expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > now(), (4) max_uses IS NULL OR current_uses < max_uses. Join with agencies table to fetch agency.name and agency.status (must be 'active' or 'approved'). Return structured error codes: TOKEN_NOT_FOUND, TOKEN_EXPIRED, TOKEN_REVOKED, TOKEN_MAX_USES_REACHED, AGENCY_INACTIVE. If valid, return agency_id, agency_name, and token.target_email (if set, verify it matches input email). Store token.id for later use in atomic increment.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with non-existent token returns TOKEN_NOT_FOUND. Test with expired token (expires_at in past) returns TOKEN_EXPIRED. Test with revoked token returns TOKEN_REVOKED. Test with token at max_uses returns TOKEN_MAX_USES_REACHED. Test valid token returns agency info.

subtasks.details: Use supabase.auth.admin.createUser() with service_role client (createClient with SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY). Parameters: email (from input), password (from input), email_confirm: true (skip email verification for invited agents), user_metadata: { first_name, last_name } (split fullName on first space). Handle AdminAuthError specifically for duplicate email (error.code === 'email_already_exists' or similar) -> return 409 EMAIL_ALREADY_EXISTS. The trigger handle_new_user (migration 0002) will automatically create the public.users record with role='user'. Store the created user.id for subsequent operations. Important: This is the first operation that creates state, so subsequent steps must handle cleanup if they fail (or use transaction).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that createUser succeeds for new email and returns user.id. Test that createUser with existing email returns appropriate error. Verify handle_new_user trigger creates public.users record. Verify user_metadata is stored correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement atomic transaction: increment token uses and insert agency_member
subtasks.description: Use Supabase RPC or raw SQL transaction to atomically increment token.current_uses and create agency_member record with role='agent'.
subtasks.details: Create a PostgreSQL function (or use raw SQL in transaction) to atomically: (1) UPDATE agency_invitation_tokens SET current_uses = current_uses + 1, updated_at = now() WHERE id = $token_id AND (max_uses IS NULL OR current_uses < max_uses) RETURNING id (if 0 rows, token was consumed by concurrent request -> rollback), (2) INSERT INTO agency_members (agency_id, user_id, member_role, status, invitation_token_id) VALUES ($agency_id, $user_id, 'agent', 'active', $token_id). The unique index agency_members_one_active_per_user ensures atomicity of 'one active membership per user' rule. If this constraint fails, user already has active membership -> return USER_ALREADY_MEMBER. Use BEGIN/COMMIT or supabase.rpc() with a pre-created function. Consider creating supabase/migrations/20260618_redeem_invitation_rpc.sql for the atomic function.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test concurrent redemptions of same token with max_uses=1 only succeeds once. Test that agency_member is created with correct role and status. Test that current_uses increments correctly. Test constraint violation for user already member of another agency.

subtasks.title: Update users.agency_id denormalization and insert user_preferences
subtasks.description: Update the users table to set agency_id (denormalized from agency_members) and create initial user_preferences record for the new agent.
subtasks.details: After successful agency_member creation: (1) UPDATE public.users SET agency_id = $agency_id, role = 'agent', updated_at = now() WHERE id = $user_id. The role change from 'user' to 'agent' enables agent-specific RLS policies. (2) INSERT INTO user_preferences (user_id) VALUES ($user_id) ON CONFLICT (user_id) DO NOTHING. This creates the 1:1 preference record for onboarding completion later. Note: agency_id on users is a denormalization for query performance (per migration 0002 comment: 'la verdad de la membresía es agency_members'). These operations should be part of the same transaction as step 4 if possible, or handle rollback if they fail.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify users.agency_id is set correctly after registration. Verify users.role changes to 'agent'. Verify user_preferences record exists for new user. Test idempotency of user_preferences insert (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING).

subtasks.title: Create user_consent record for latest terms_versions

subtasks.details: Query terms_versions for current documents: SELECT id, doc_type FROM terms_versions WHERE is_current = true. Insert into user_consents for each required consent_type: (1) consent_type='terms' with terms_version_id of current 'terms' doc, (2) consent_type='privacy' with terms_version_id of current 'privacy' doc, (3) consent_type='age' (no version required, user confirms 18+). Set accepted_at = now() and optionally capture ip_address from request headers (X-Forwarded-For). Per migration 0004, user_consents is immutable (append-only via RLS). Return success response with the created user info.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify user_consents records are created for terms, privacy, and age. Verify correct terms_version_id is linked. Verify accepted_at timestamp is set. Test with missing terms_versions (should handle gracefully or fail with clear error).

subtasks.details: In the Edge Function: After all operations succeed, call supabase.auth.signInWithPassword(email, password) to generate a session, or use admin.generateLink() for a magic link. Return { user, session } (access_token, refresh_token) so the client can immediately use them. In app/register.tsx: Map error codes to Spanish user-friendly messages: TOKEN_NOT_FOUND='Código de invitación no válido', TOKEN_EXPIRED='Este código ha expirado', TOKEN_MAX_USES_REACHED='Este código ya fue utilizado el máximo de veces', EMAIL_ALREADY_EXISTS='Ya existe una cuenta con este correo', USER_ALREADY_MEMBER='Ya perteneces a otra inmobiliaria'. On success: Store session using the AuthContext from task 2 (setSession or supabase.auth.setSession), then navigate to the main app (router.replace('/(tabs)')). Handle network errors with retry option. Add haptic feedback on success (Haptics.notificationAsync).

If user is not authenticated, redirect to /login. If authenticated but role !== 'admin', redirect to home (/(tabs)). Use Slot from Expo Router for nested screens. Add a simple admin header with 'Admin Panel' title and back navigation. Style: clean, utilitarian (light background, standard fonts) per PRD §8 guidance. Consider adding a loading state while checking auth status.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Unauthenticated users redirect to /login, (2) Users with role='user' or 'agent' redirect to home with 403-style message, (3) Users with role='admin' can access nested admin routes, (4) Layout renders correctly with header and Slot

subtasks.title: Build agency list screen (app/admin/agencies.tsx)
subtasks.description: Create screen showing all agencies with their status, owner info, and navigation to create new agencies
subtasks.details: Create app/admin/agencies.tsx (or index.tsx if default route). Query: SELECT agencies.*, users.first_name, users.last_name, users.email FROM agencies LEFT JOIN agency_members ON agencies.id = agency_members.agency_id AND agency_members.member_role = 'owner' LEFT JOIN users ON agency_members.user_id = users.id WHERE agencies.deleted_at IS NULL ORDER BY agencies.created_at DESC. Display: agency name, status badge (pending_approval/approved/active/suspended/rejected using agency_status enum), owner name/email, created_at. Add FAB or header button to navigate to /admin/agencies/create. Use FlatList with pull-to-refresh. Empty state: 'No agencies yet. Create the first one.'
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) All agencies display with correct data, (2) Status badges show correct colors, (3) Owner info displays correctly, (4) Navigation to create screen works, (5) Pull-to-refresh reloads data, (6) Empty state shows when no agencies

subtasks.title: Create agency form screen (app/admin/agencies/create.tsx)
subtasks.description: Build form for creating new agencies with name, slug, contact info fields and validation
subtasks.details: Create app/admin/agencies/create.tsx with form fields: name (required, citext), slug (auto-generated from name, editable, validated for URL-safety), contact_name (text), contact_phone (text), contact_email (email validation). Use controlled inputs with useState. Add form validation: name required (min 2 chars), slug unique format (lowercase, hyphens only), email format if provided. Show validation errors inline. Submit button calls Edge Function (subtask 4). Include owner section: owner_email (required, for creating the owner user), owner_first_name, owner_last_name. Style: clean form layout, grouped sections (Agency Info, Contact Info, Owner Info).
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) All fields render and are editable, (2) Slug auto-generates from name, (3) Validation errors show for invalid inputs, (4) Submit disabled when required fields empty, (5) Form data correctly formatted for API call

subtasks.title: Create Edge Function admin-create-agency with admin role verification
subtasks.description: Build supabase/functions/admin-create-agency/index.ts to verify admin role and create agency record atomically
subtasks.details: Create supabase/functions/admin-create-agency/index.ts. Request body: { agency: { name, slug, contact_name, contact_phone, contact_email }, owner: { email, first_name, last_name } }. Steps: (1) Extract JWT from Authorization header, verify with Supabase Auth, (2) Query public.users WHERE id = auth.uid() to verify role = 'admin', return 403 if not, (3) Validate inputs (name required, slug format, email formats), (4) Begin transaction or use service_role client, (5) INSERT INTO agencies (name, slug, contact_name, contact_phone, contact_email, status, created_by_user_id) VALUES (..., 'active', admin_id) RETURNING id. Use _shared/ utilities for auth verification and standard response format. Return agency.id on success.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Non-authenticated requests return 401, (2) Non-admin users return 403, (3) Admin users can create agency, (4) Agency record created with correct data, (5) Invalid inputs return 400 with validation errors

subtasks.title: Implement owner user creation via Auth Admin API with agency_member insertion
subtasks.description: Extend Edge Function to create owner user via Supabase Auth Admin API and insert agency_member with role='owner'
subtasks.details: Extend admin-create-agency Edge Function (subtask 4). After agency creation: (1) Use supabase.auth.admin.createUser({ email: owner.email, email_confirm: true, user_metadata: { first_name, last_name } }) to create owner in auth.users (triggers handle_new_user for public.users), (2) Update public.users SET role = 'agent', agency_id = agency.id WHERE id = new_user.id, (3) INSERT INTO agency_members (agency_id, user_id, member_role, status) VALUES (agency.id, new_user.id, 'owner', 'active'), (4) Generate temporary password or magic link (for demo: generate random password and return it, or set a known test password). All operations use service_role key. Return { agency_id, owner_user_id, temporary_credentials } for UI display.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Owner user created in auth.users, (2) public.users record has role='agent' and correct agency_id, (3) agency_member record exists with member_role='owner', (4) Temporary credentials returned, (5) Rollback on failure (no orphan records)

subtasks.title: Generate initial invitation token for owner and log to admin_actions
subtasks.description: Create invitation token for the owner to invite agents, and log the agency creation action to admin_actions audit table
subtasks.details: Extend Edge Function: After owner creation (subtask 5): (1) Generate secure token: crypto.randomUUID() or similar, (2) Hash token for storage: use crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', ...) and store hex, (3) INSERT INTO agency_invitation_tokens (agency_id, token, max_uses, expires_at, created_by_user_id) VALUES (agency.id, hashed_token, 10, now() + interval '30 days', owner_user.id), (4) INSERT INTO admin_actions (admin_id, action_type, entity_type, entity_id, new_values, reason) VALUES (admin.id, 'create_agency_with_owner', 'agency', agency.id, { agency_name, owner_email }, 'Created via admin panel'). Return the plain token (unhashed) to UI for display. The token format: 8-char alphanumeric for easy sharing.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Invitation token record created with hashed value, (2) Plain token returned to UI is different from stored hash, (3) Token has correct max_uses and expires_at, (4) admin_actions log entry created with correct data, (5) Token can be validated later (hash matches)

subtasks.title: Build invitation code display screen with copy button and agency members list
subtasks.description: Create screen showing generated invitation codes with copy functionality and list of agency members with their status
subtasks.details: Create app/admin/agencies/[id].tsx (agency detail screen). Sections: (1) Agency Info: name, status, contact details, created_at. (2) Owner Info: name, email, role. (3) Invitation Codes: query agency_invitation_tokens WHERE agency_id = id, display each with: token (masked except last 4 chars), max_uses, current_uses, expires_at, status (active/expired/revoked). Add 'Copy Code' button using Clipboard.setStringAsync(). (4) Agency Members: query agency_members JOIN users, display member name, email, member_role (owner/agent), status, joined_at. Use FlatList for members. Add button to generate new invitation code (POST to Edge Function). Style: card-based layout, status badges, utilitarian design. After successful agency creation (subtask 3 submit), navigate to this screen showing the generated credentials.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Agency details display correctly, (2) Invitation codes list with correct data, (3) Copy button copies plain token to clipboard, (4) Members list shows all agency members, (5) Navigation from create screen works, (6) Generate new code button creates new token

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down admin panel into: (1) create admin layout with role guard, (2) build agency list screen, (3) create agency form screen, (4) create Edge Function admin-create-agency with admin role verification, (5) implement owner user creation via Auth Admin API with agency_member insertion, (6) generate initial invitation token and log to admin_actions, (7) build invitation code display and agency members list screen

tasks.title: Build 3-step property publication wizard
tasks.description: Create multi-step wizard for publishing properties: (1) operation/type, (2) details/location, (3) video upload
tasks.details:
1. Create wizard layout (app/publish/_layout.tsx) with step indicator (1/3, 2/3, 3/3)
2. Step 1 (app/publish/step1.tsx): Select operation_type (rent/sale) and property_type (house/apartment/etc)
3. Step 2 (app/publish/step2.tsx): Price, bedrooms, bathrooms, square_meters, address autocomplete with Google Places API, map picker for exact location (react-native-maps), pet_friendly, allows_no_guarantor, student_friendly checkboxes, description textarea
4. Step 3 (app/publish/step3.tsx): Video upload with expo-document-picker (video filter), show preview, upload to storage bucket, display progress bar
5. Form state management with React Context or Zustand across steps
6. Edge Function: supabase/functions/publish-property/index.ts
7. Logic: (a) Validate all required fields, (b) Create property record with status='active' (auto-approved for demo), (c) Create property_videos record with storage_path, status='ready', (d) Set published_at to now(), (e) Return property_id
8. Back/Next navigation between steps with validation
9. Draft saving (optional, nice-to-have)
10. Test: Complete flow creates property with video
tasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Can navigate through all 3 steps, (2) Validation prevents advancing with missing required fields, (3) Address autocomplete works, (4) Map picker sets correct location, (5) Video uploads successfully, (6) Property created with status='active', (7) property_videos created with correct storage_path, (8) Can view published property in feed immediately

subtasks.title: Create wizard layout with step indicator and navigation
subtasks.description: Build the wizard container layout (app/publish/_layout.tsx) with a step indicator showing current progress (1/3, 2/3, 3/3) and shared navigation logic for back/next between steps.
subtasks.details: Create app/publish/_layout.tsx using Expo Router's Stack navigator. Implement a StepIndicator component showing 3 steps with visual distinction for current/completed/pending states. Include a header with back button that navigates to previous step (or exits wizard on step 1). Use Slot from expo-router to render child screens. Export shared types for wizard navigation props. Follow the PRD hybrid design: light mode for publication screens. Structure: app/publish/ with _layout.tsx, step1.tsx, step2.tsx, step3.tsx.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Step indicator displays correctly on each screen, (2) Back button works on each step, (3) Layout renders child screens properly, (4) Visual styles match hybrid design (light mode).

subtasks.title: Build Step 1 for operation and property type selection
subtasks.description: Create Step 1 screen (app/publish/step1.tsx) allowing users to select operation_type (rent/sale) and property_type from the available enums in the database schema.
subtasks.details: Create app/publish/step1.tsx with two selection groups: operation_type (rent/sale using operation_type enum) and property_type (house, apartment, land, commercial, room using property_type enum). Use visually distinct cards or buttons for selection (tap to select, highlight selected). Both fields required before enabling Next button. Property types should map to the DB enum: house, apartment, land, commercial, room. Include clear labels in Spanish matching the PRD. Connect to form state context (will be created in subtask 7). Add Next button that validates selection and navigates to step2.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Both operation types display and are selectable, (2) All property types display and are selectable, (3) Cannot advance without selecting both, (4) Selection persists when navigating back from step 2.

subtasks.title: Build Step 2 base with price and property details inputs
subtasks.description: Create Step 2 screen (app/publish/step2.tsx) with input fields for price, bedrooms, bathrooms, square_meters, and description textarea.
subtasks.details: Create app/publish/step2.tsx with a scrollable form containing: price (numeric input, required, format with currency), bedrooms (numeric picker 0-10+), bathrooms (numeric picker 0-10+), square_meters (numeric input, optional), description (multiline textarea, optional, max 2000 chars). Use controlled inputs connected to form state. Add input validation (price > 0, bedrooms/bathrooms >= 0). Include clear labels and placeholders in Spanish. Make the form scrollable to accommodate all fields plus location section. Style inputs consistently with the light-mode design.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) All numeric inputs accept valid values and reject invalid, (2) Price displays formatted correctly, (3) Description textarea works with character limit, (4) Form scrolls properly on smaller screens.

subtasks.title: Integrate Google Places API for address autocomplete
subtasks.description: Add Google Places autocomplete to Step 2 for address input, storing structured address data (address, state, city, zone) and initial coordinates.
subtasks.details: Add a text input with Google Places Autocomplete integration. Use react-native-google-places-autocomplete or similar library compatible with Expo dev builds. Configure with API key from environment variables (EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY). When user selects an address: (1) set address field with formatted address, (2) extract and set state, city, zone (colonia) from address_components, (3) set initial location coordinates from place geometry. Limit results to Mexico (country restriction) per the PRD context. Handle API errors gracefully with user feedback. Store the selected place data in form state for map picker refinement.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Autocomplete suggestions appear as user types, (2) Selecting a place populates address, state, city, zone fields, (3) Coordinates are extracted correctly, (4) Works offline gracefully (shows error, allows manual entry later).

subtasks.title: Implement map picker for exact location selection
subtasks.description: Add an interactive map picker using react-native-maps to let users refine or set the exact property location with a draggable marker.
subtasks.details: Add react-native-maps MapView component below the address input in Step 2. Initial map center: coordinates from Google Places selection, or default (Mexico City center) if not yet selected. Include a draggable marker for exact location placement. When marker is moved, update the location coordinates in form state. Show current coordinates as small text below map (for debugging/verification). Map should be a reasonable height (200-250px) and expand to show clearly. Use Google Maps provider (via Expo dev build). Store final coordinates as { latitude, longitude } in form state, to be converted to PostGIS geography on submission.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Map renders with correct initial position, (2) Marker is draggable and updates coordinates, (3) Map centers on address when selected from autocomplete, (4) Works with Expo dev build (native Google Maps).

subtasks.title: Add niche filters toggles and complete Step 2 validation
subtasks.description: Add pet_friendly, allows_no_guarantor, student_friendly toggle switches to Step 2 and implement complete validation before advancing to Step 3.
subtasks.details: Add three toggle/switch components in Step 2: pet_friendly (Se aceptan mascotas), allows_no_guarantor (Sin aval), student_friendly (Ideal para estudiantes). Default all to false. Add validation logic for Step 2: price required and > 0, address required (must have selected from autocomplete or map), location coordinates required. Show validation errors inline or as a toast when user tries to advance without required fields. Next button navigates to step3 only when validation passes. Ensure all Step 2 data is properly stored in form state before navigation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) All three toggles work and persist state, (2) Cannot advance without price, (3) Cannot advance without address/location, (4) Validation errors display clearly, (5) Valid form advances to Step 3.

subtasks.title: Implement form state management with Context/Zustand
subtasks.description: Create centralized form state management using React Context or Zustand to persist wizard data across all three steps with validation helpers.
subtasks.details: Create a PublishFormProvider (Context) or Zustand store at src/features/publish/store/ or embedded in the publish route. State shape: { operationType, propertyType, price, bedrooms, bathrooms, squareMeters, description, address, state, city, zone, location: {lat, lng}, petFriendly, allowsNoGuarantor, studentFriendly, videoUri, videoFileName }. Include actions: setStep1Data, setStep2Data, setStep3Data, resetForm, getPropertyPayload (transforms to API shape). Add validation functions: validateStep1, validateStep2, validateStep3. Wrap publish routes with provider in _layout.tsx. Ensure state persists through navigation between steps. Include isSubmitting flag for submission state.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) State persists when navigating between steps, (2) Reset clears all data, (3) getPropertyPayload returns correct shape for API, (4) Validation functions work correctly.

subtasks.title: Build Step 3 with video upload and preview
subtasks.description: Create Step 3 screen (app/publish/step3.tsx) with video selection using expo-document-picker, video preview, and upload to Supabase Storage with progress bar.
subtasks.details: Create app/publish/step3.tsx with: (1) Video picker button using expo-document-picker with type filter for video (video/*), (2) Preview using expo-video or react-native-video showing selected video, (3) Upload button that uploads to Supabase Storage bucket 'property-videos' with path format: {userId}/{propertyId}/{timestamp}.mp4, (4) Progress bar showing upload percentage using Supabase Storage upload with onUploadProgress, (5) Validation: video required before publish. Limit video size (e.g., 100MB) and duration if possible to check before upload. Show file name and size after selection. Handle upload errors with retry option. Store storage_path in form state after successful upload.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Video picker opens and filters to video files, (2) Selected video previews correctly, (3) Upload shows progress, (4) Upload succeeds and returns storage_path, (5) Handles large files and errors gracefully.

subtasks.title: Create Edge Function for property publication
subtasks.description: Build Edge Function supabase/functions/publish-property/index.ts that validates all fields, creates property with status='active', creates property_videos record, and returns property_id.
subtasks.details: Create supabase/functions/publish-property/index.ts following lineamientos: (1) Authenticate user via Supabase auth, (2) Validate payload with Zod schema matching properties table: operation_type, property_type, address, location (convert lat/lng to PostGIS), price, bedrooms, bathrooms, square_meters, description, pet_friendly, allows_no_guarantor, student_friendly, storage_path, (3) Get user's agency_id from agency_members if applicable, (4) Insert into properties with status='active', published_at=now(), owner_user_id=auth.uid(), (5) Insert into property_videos with storage_path, status='ready', position=1, (6) Return { property_id, success: true }. Use service_role for insert. Include proper error responses (400 for validation, 401 for auth, 500 for DB errors). Add _shared/ imports for auth validation and response helpers.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Rejects unauthenticated requests with 401, (2) Validates all required fields, (3) Creates property with correct data, (4) Creates property_videos with storage_path, (5) Returns property_id on success, (6) Handles validation errors with clear messages.

subtasks.title: Connect wizard to Edge Function and test complete flow
subtasks.description: Wire Step 3 publish button to call the Edge Function, handle response, show success/error states, and test the complete publication flow end-to-end.
subtasks.details: In Step 3, add publish button that: (1) Shows loading state during submission, (2) Calls getPropertyPayload from form state, (3) Invokes publish-property Edge Function via supabase.functions.invoke, (4) On success: show success message, reset form, navigate to property detail or my-publications, (5) On error: show error message, allow retry. Convert location coordinates to PostGIS format in payload: ST_Point(lng, lat). Handle network errors distinctly from validation errors. Add Supabase client function wrapper at src/lib/supabase/functions.ts for type-safe function calls. Test complete flow: step1 selection → step2 details → step3 video upload → publish → verify property created in DB with status='active' and property_videos record.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Can navigate through all 3 steps, (2) Validation prevents advancing with missing fields, (3) Address autocomplete works, (4) Map picker sets correct location, (5) Video uploads successfully, (6) Property created with status='active', (7) property_videos created with correct storage_path.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down publication wizard into: (1) create wizard layout with step indicator, (2) build step 1 for operation/property type selection, (3) build step 2 base with price and property details, (4) integrate Google Places API for address autocomplete, (5) implement map picker for exact location, (6) add niche filters toggles and step 2 validation, (7) implement form state management with Context/Zustand, (8) build step 3 with video upload and preview, (9) create Edge Function for property publication, (10) connect wizard to Edge Function and test complete flow

tasks.title: Implement vertical video feed with swipe and autoplay
tasks.description: Create TikTok-style vertical video feed showing active properties with expo-video, swipe navigation, and autoplay
tasks.details:
1. Create feed screen (app/(tabs)/index.tsx) as main home tab
2. Use FlatList with pagingEnabled and vertical scroll
3. Each item full-screen video player using expo-video (Video component)
4. Implement viewability logic: autoplay video when 70% visible, pause when scrolled away
5. Preload next 2 videos for smooth scrolling
6. Query properties: status='active', deleted_at is null, join property_videos where status='ready', order by created_at desc, paginate with limit=10 + cursor
7. Overlay UI: Property info card at bottom (price, address, bedrooms/bathrooms), like/save buttons (floating), agent profile picture (tap to view profile)
8. Double-tap to like gesture
9. Swipe up to next video, swipe down to previous
10. Loading skeleton while videos load
11. Pull-to-refresh for new properties
12. Test: Smooth scrolling, autoplay works, preloading prevents lag
tasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Videos autoplay when in view, (2) Smooth swipe between videos, (3) Double-tap like works, (4) Property info displays correctly, (5) Pagination loads more properties, (6) Pull-to-refresh fetches new properties, (7) Memory doesn't leak with many videos, (8) Preloading improves perceived performance

subtasks.title: Create feed screen with FlatList and vertical paging
subtasks.description: Set up the main feed screen at app/(tabs)/index.tsx as the home tab with a vertically scrolling FlatList using pagingEnabled for full-screen snap behavior.
subtasks.details: Create app/(tabs)/index.tsx as the home tab screen. Implement a FlatList with pagingEnabled=true, showsVerticalScrollIndicator=false, and decelerationRate='fast' for TikTok-style snapping. Each item should occupy full screen height using useWindowDimensions(). Set up proper TypeScript types for the feed item data structure matching the properties + property_videos schema (id, price, address, bedrooms, bathrooms, owner_user_id, agency_id, playback_url, thumbnail_url). Use keyExtractor with property.id. Add getItemLayout for performance optimization since all items have fixed height. Configure initialNumToRender=2 and maxToRenderPerBatch=3 for memory efficiency.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that feed screen loads as home tab, FlatList renders with vertical scrolling, and items snap to full screen boundaries on swipe.

subtasks.title: Implement full-screen video player with expo-video
subtasks.description: Create a VideoFeedItem component using expo-video's VideoView for each feed item, displaying property videos at full screen with proper aspect ratio handling.
subtasks.details: Create components/feed/VideoFeedItem.tsx using expo-video (not expo-av). Import { VideoView, useVideoPlayer } from 'expo-video'. Each video player should fill the screen with resizeMode='cover' for vertical videos. Accept props: playbackUrl, thumbnailUrl, isActive (controls playback), onVideoEnd. Initialize the player with useVideoPlayer(playbackUrl, player => { player.loop = true }). Set player.play() or player.pause() based on isActive prop using useEffect. Add poster image using thumbnail_url while video loads. Handle errors gracefully with fallback UI. Style container with position: 'absolute', backgroundColor: '#000' for immersive dark feed aesthetic per PRD.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that videos render at full screen, play when isActive=true, pause when isActive=false, loop correctly, and display thumbnails while loading.

subtasks.title: Implement viewability detection for autoplay/pause
subtasks.description: Add viewability logic to autoplay videos when 70% visible and pause when scrolled away, tracking the currently active video index.
subtasks.details: Implement viewabilityConfig on FlatList: { itemVisiblePercentThreshold: 70, minimumViewTime: 100 }. Use onViewableItemsChanged callback with useCallback to track which item is most visible. Store activeIndex in useState. Pass isActive={index === activeIndex} prop to each VideoFeedItem. Use viewabilityConfigCallbackPairs ref pattern to avoid FlatList remounting issues. Handle edge cases: pause all when app goes to background using AppState listener from react-native. Resume playback when returning to foreground. Add useFocusEffect from @react-navigation/native to pause when navigating away from feed tab.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that videos autoplay when scrolled into view at 70%+ visibility, pause when scrolled away, pause on app background, and resume on foreground.

subtasks.title: Implement video preloading for smooth scrolling
subtasks.description: Preload the next 2 videos ahead of the current position to ensure smooth scrolling without loading delays.
subtasks.details: Create a preloading strategy using expo-video's prefetch capabilities. Maintain a preloadedVideos ref tracking which URLs have been preloaded. When activeIndex changes, trigger preload for indices activeIndex+1 and activeIndex+2. Use expo-video's Asset.loadAsync or equivalent prefetch method if available, otherwise implement by creating hidden VideoPlayer instances that load but don't render. Implement windowSize={5} on FlatList to keep 5 items mounted (2 before, current, 2 after). Add removeClippedSubviews={true} for Android performance. Track preload state per video to show loading indicator if video at current index isn't ready yet.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that scrolling to next/previous videos shows no loading delay, preloading occurs for upcoming videos, and memory stays bounded.

subtasks.title: Create Supabase query for active properties with videos
subtasks.description: Implement the data fetching layer to query active properties with ready videos, including cursor-based pagination with limit=10.
subtasks.details: Create lib/queries/feedProperties.ts with async function fetchFeedProperties(cursor?: string). Query: select id, price, address, bedrooms, bathrooms, owner_user_id, agency_id, created_at, property_videos(id, playback_url, thumbnail_url, position) from properties where status='active' and deleted_at is null and exists(select 1 from property_videos pv where pv.property_id = properties.id and pv.status='ready' and pv.deleted_at is null) order by created_at desc limit 10. If cursor provided, add filter: created_at < cursor. Return { data: Property[], nextCursor: string | null }. Use Supabase nested select syntax per lineamientos §5.13. Add TypeScript types for FeedProperty matching the query shape. Handle Supabase errors and return typed response.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test query returns active properties with ready videos, pagination works with cursor, and returns empty array gracefully when no more items.

subtasks.title: Build overlay UI with property info card and action buttons
subtasks.description: Create the overlay UI showing property information (price, address, bedrooms/bathrooms) at the bottom and floating like/save buttons.
subtasks.details: Create components/feed/PropertyOverlay.tsx with props: property (price, address, bedrooms, bathrooms, owner), onLike, onSave, isLiked, isSaved, onAgentPress. Bottom card: semi-transparent dark gradient background (LinearGradient from expo-linear-gradient), display formatted price (Intl.NumberFormat for MXN currency), address, bed/bath icons with counts. Right side floating buttons: heart icon for like (outline/filled based on isLiked), bookmark icon for save (outline/filled based on isSaved). Agent avatar in bottom-right corner, tappable to view profile. Use absolute positioning over the video. Apply safe area insets for notch/home indicator. Style per PRD: feed is dark and immersive, use white text with shadow for readability.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test overlay displays property info correctly, buttons show correct state, taps trigger callbacks, and UI is readable over various video content.

subtasks.title: Implement double-tap to like gesture
subtasks.description: Add double-tap gesture recognition on the video to trigger like action with visual heart animation feedback.
subtasks.details: Wrap VideoFeedItem in react-native-gesture-handler's TapGestureHandler with numberOfTaps={2}. On double-tap: trigger onLike callback, show animated heart icon in center of screen using react-native-reanimated (fade in, scale up, then fade out after 800ms). Track last tap timestamp to differentiate from single taps. Ensure gesture doesn't conflict with scroll gestures. Add haptic feedback using expo-haptics (Haptics.impactAsync(ImpactFeedbackStyle.Light)). If already liked, double-tap should still show animation but not unlike (matches TikTok behavior). Create HeartAnimation component with Animated.View and opacity/scale animations.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test double-tap triggers like callback, shows heart animation, provides haptic feedback, and doesn't interfere with scrolling.

subtasks.title: Add swipe navigation between videos
subtasks.description: Ensure swipe up advances to next video and swipe down returns to previous, with smooth snap behavior.
subtasks.details: FlatList with pagingEnabled already handles basic swipe-to-snap. Fine-tune with snapToAlignment='start' and snapToInterval={screenHeight}. Add velocity threshold detection: if swipe velocity > threshold, scroll to next/previous item even if drag distance is small. Use onScrollEndDrag and onMomentumScrollEnd to detect final position and update activeIndex. Implement scrollToIndex for programmatic navigation if needed. Add bounces={false} or limited bounce on edges. Ensure smooth 60fps animation by optimizing render cycle - use React.memo on VideoFeedItem, avoid re-renders during scroll using useCallback for all handlers.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test swipe up advances to next video, swipe down returns to previous, snapping is smooth at 60fps, and edge bouncing is minimal.

subtasks.title: Implement loading skeleton, pull-to-refresh, and memory management
subtasks.description: Add loading skeleton while initial data loads, pull-to-refresh for new properties, and proper memory management to prevent leaks with many videos.
subtasks.details: Create components/feed/FeedSkeleton.tsx: full-screen dark placeholder with shimmer animation (using react-native-skeleton-placeholder or custom LinearGradient animation) showing ghost UI for video area and property card. subtasks.title: Fetch active properties and convert PostGIS geography to lat/lng
subtasks.description: Query active properties with location data from Supabase and transform PostGIS geography(Point, 4326) to latitude/longitude coordinates for markers.
subtasks.details: Create hook useMapProperties() in lib/hooks/ that fetches properties with: SELECT id, price, location, operation_type, property_type, bedrooms, bathrooms, address FROM properties WHERE status='active' AND deleted_at IS NULL. PostGIS geography is stored as Point(4326) - use ST_Y(location::geometry) for latitude and ST_X(location::geometry) for longitude in the query, or extract from GeoJSON if Supabase returns it that way. Define TypeScript interface MapProperty with id, price, lat, lng, operationType, propertyType, bedrooms, bathrooms, address. Handle loading/error states. Cache results appropriately.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify query returns correct properties with coordinates. Test with mock data to ensure PostGIS conversion is accurate. Confirm lat/lng values fall within expected Guadalajara region bounds.

Configure clustering options: radius=50 (pixels), maxZoom=16, minPoints=2 (minimum markers to form cluster). Style cluster markers to show count badge with property count. Implement onClusterPress to zoom into cluster bounds. Handle cluster expansion animation. Ensure clustering recalculates on zoom changes. Test performance with 50+ markers to ensure smooth interaction without UI jank.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test clustering behavior at different zoom levels. Verify clusters form when markers are close together and expand when zoomed in. Performance test with 50+ property markers.

subtasks.title: Create Edge Function toggle-like with optimistic count update
subtasks.description: Implement supabase/functions/toggle-like/index.ts Edge Function to insert/delete in likes table and atomically update property.like_count
subtasks.details: Create supabase/functions/toggle-like/index.ts: (1) POST endpoint accepting { property_video_id: string, property_id: string }, (2) Get current user from auth.getUser(), return 401 if not authenticated, (3) Check if like exists: SELECT id FROM likes WHERE user_id = current_user AND property_video_id = input, (4) If exists: DELETE from likes, decrement properties.like_count WHERE id = property_id using UPDATE ... SET like_count = like_count - 1, (5) If not exists: INSERT into likes (user_id, property_video_id, property_id), increment like_count + 1, (6) Wrap in transaction for atomicity, (7) Return { liked: boolean, likeCount: number }, (8) Handle edge cases: video not found, property not found, negative count guard (GREATEST(like_count - 1, 0)). Use _shared/ patterns for auth validation and response formatting.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Like creates record and increments count, (2) Unlike deletes record and decrements count, (3) Double-like is idempotent, (4) Unauthenticated request returns 401, (5) Transaction rolls back on partial failure, (6) Count never goes negative

Export from src/components/index.ts. Consider sharing animation logic with LikeButton via a custom hook useToggleAnimation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Bookmark icon renders in both states, (2) Animation plays smoothly on toggle, (3) Color changes correctly, (4) onToggle fires with correct params, (5) Disabled state works

subtasks.title: Create Edge Function toggle-save with optimistic count update
subtasks.description: Implement supabase/functions/toggle-save/index.ts Edge Function to insert/delete in saves table and atomically update property.save_count
subtasks.details: Create supabase/functions/toggle-save/index.ts: (1) POST endpoint accepting { property_id: string }, (2) Get current user from auth.getUser(), return 401 if not authenticated, (3) Check if save exists: SELECT id FROM saves WHERE user_id = current_user AND property_id = input, (4) If exists: DELETE from saves, decrement properties.save_count using UPDATE ... SET save_count = GREATEST(save_count - 1, 0), (5) If not exists: INSERT into saves (user_id, property_id), increment save_count + 1, (6) Wrap in transaction for atomicity, (7) Return { saved: boolean, saveCount: number }, (8) Handle edge cases: property not found (404), negative count guard. Use _shared/ patterns consistent with toggle-like.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Save creates record and increments count, (2) Unsave deletes record and decrements count, (3) Double-save is idempotent, (4) Unauthenticated request returns 401, (5) Transaction atomicity on failure, (6) Count never negative

subtasks.title: Implement optimistic UI updates with error rollback
subtasks.description: Create hooks useLike and useSave that provide optimistic updates with immediate visual feedback and automatic rollback on server error
subtasks.details: Create src/hooks/useLike.ts and src/hooks/useSave.ts: (1) useLike({ propertyVideoId, propertyId, initialLiked, initialCount }) returns { isLiked, likeCount, toggleLike, isLoading }, (2) toggleLike immediately updates local state (optimistic), calls toggle-like Edge Function, on error reverts state and shows toast, (3) useSave({ propertyId, initialSaved, initialCount }) returns { isSaved, saveCount, toggleSave, isLoading }, (4) Same optimistic pattern with rollback, (5) Use try/catch with state reversion in catch block, (6) Optional: debounce rapid toggles (300ms) to prevent spam, (7) Integrate with react-query or zustand if already in codebase for cache invalidation, otherwise use local useState. Create src/lib/api/engagement.ts with typed functions callToggleLike and callToggleSave.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Optimistic update reflects immediately, (2) On success state persists, (3) On network error state reverts, (4) Loading state shows during request, (5) Rapid toggles are debounced, (6) Error toast displays on failure

tasks.title: Create WhatsApp contact with automatic lead generation

tasks.details:
1. Create contact button component on property detail and feed overlay
2. Edge Function: supabase/functions/contact-agent/index.ts
3. Input: { propertyId: string }
4. Logic: (a) Get property and agent info, (b) Check if lead already exists (leads table unique on agent_id + user_id), (c) If not exists, insert lead with status='new', (d) Insert lead_origin_properties linking lead to property, (e) Increment property.contact_count, (f) Return agent phone number and pre-filled message
5. Pre-filled WhatsApp message template: 'Hola [Agent Name], vi tu propiedad en Urbea: [Property Address] - [Price]. Me gustaría recibir más información.'
6. Open WhatsApp using Linking.openURL(`whatsapp://send?phone=${phone}&text=${encodeURIComponent(message)}`)
7. Handle case where WhatsApp not installed (show error or fallback to SMS)
8. Idempotent: multiple taps don't create duplicate leads
9. Show toast confirmation: 'Contacto enviado'
10. Test: Contact creates lead, opens WhatsApp, no duplicates
tasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Contact button creates lead in database, (2) WhatsApp opens with correct pre-filled message, (3) Duplicate contacts don't create duplicate leads, (4) Lead shows correct property in lead_origin_properties, (5) property.contact_count increments, (6) Works for both feed and detail screen, (7) Error handling when WhatsApp not installed, (8) Agent phone number is correct

subtasks.title: Implement property and agent info retrieval with validation
subtasks.description: Add logic to fetch property details and associated agent information, validating property exists and is active with a valid agent phone number.
subtasks.details: In contact-agent Edge Function: Query properties table joining with users (owner) to get agent info. SELECT p.id, p.address, p.price, p.status, p.owner_user_id, u.display_name as agent_name, u.phone as agent_phone FROM properties p JOIN users u ON p.owner_user_id = u.id WHERE p.id = propertyId AND p.deleted_at IS NULL. Validate: property exists (404 if not), status = 'active' (400 if not contactable), agent has phone number (400 if missing). Return descriptive errors for each validation failure.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Returns 404 for non-existent property, (2) Returns 400 for non-active property status, (3) Returns 400 if agent has no phone, (4) Returns property/agent data for valid active property with agent phone.

subtasks.title: Implement idempotent lead creation with unique constraint check
subtasks.description: Add logic to check if lead exists for agent_id + user_id pair, create new lead if not exists, ensuring idempotency per the unique index leads_agent_user_unique_active.
subtasks.details: Query leads table: SELECT id FROM leads WHERE agent_id = property.owner_user_id AND user_id = auth.uid() AND deleted_at IS NULL. If lead exists, store lead_id for later use (no duplicate creation). If not exists, INSERT INTO leads (agent_id, user_id, status, first_contact_at) VALUES (owner_user_id, auth_user_id, 'new', now()) RETURNING id. Use the existing unique index leads_agent_user_unique_active for idempotency guarantee. Handle race condition by catching unique constraint violation and re-querying existing lead.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) First contact creates new lead with status='new', (2) Second contact on same agent returns existing lead_id without creating duplicate, (3) Concurrent requests don't create duplicates (constraint violation handled gracefully).

subtasks.title: Insert lead_origin_properties and increment property.contact_count
subtasks.description: Link the lead to the originating property via lead_origin_properties table and atomically increment the property's contact_count counter.
subtasks.details: After obtaining lead_id (new or existing): INSERT INTO lead_origin_properties (lead_id, property_id, contacted_at) VALUES (lead_id, propertyId, now()) ON CONFLICT (lead_id, property_id) DO NOTHING. This handles idempotency if user contacts same property multiple times. Then atomically increment counter: UPDATE properties SET contact_count = contact_count + 1 WHERE id = propertyId. Note: per lineamientos §6.4, counters updated in write-time via Edge Functions. Only increment if this is a NEW lead_origin_properties record (use INSERT ... RETURNING or check affected rows).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) New contact creates lead_origin_properties record, (2) Duplicate contact on same property doesn't duplicate lead_origin_properties, (3) contact_count increments only on first contact per property, (4) Multiple properties from same lead each get their own lead_origin_properties entry.

subtasks.title: Generate pre-filled WhatsApp message and return response

subtasks.details: Build message template: 'Hola [Agent Name], vi tu propiedad en Urbea: [Property Address] - [Price]. Me gustaría recibir más información.' Format price with Mexican locale (e.g., '$1,500,000 MXN' for sale or '$15,000/mes' for rent). Sanitize agent name and address to prevent injection (remove special chars that could break URL encoding). Return JSON response: { success: true, phone: agent_phone (with country code, e.g., '+52...'), message: prefilledMessage }. Phone should be digits only for WhatsApp URL.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) Message contains correct agent name, address, and price, (2) Price formatted correctly for rent vs sale operation types, (3) Phone returned in correct format (digits only with country code), (4) Special characters in address/name sanitized properly.

subtasks.title: Implement WhatsApp deep link opening with fallback and toast confirmation
subtasks.description: Handle WhatsApp opening via Linking.openURL with proper encoding, fallback to SMS if WhatsApp not installed, and show toast confirmation on success.
subtasks.details: In ContactAgentButton component, after receiving Edge Function response: Build URL whatsapp://send?phone=${phone}&text=${encodeURIComponent(message)}. Use Linking.canOpenURL('whatsapp://send') to check if WhatsApp installed. If yes, call Linking.openURL(whatsappUrl). Handle Linking.openURL errors gracefully (show error toast). Add analytics event for contact_initiated with propertyId.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test: (1) WhatsApp opens with correct pre-filled message when installed, (2) Falls back to SMS when WhatsApp not available, (3) Toast 'Contacto enviado' shows on success, (4) Error toast shows if opening fails, (5) Multiple rapid taps don't trigger multiple Edge Function calls (debounce/loading state).

subtasks.title: Create Edge Function update-lead-status with validation
subtasks.description: Create supabase/functions/update-lead-status/index.ts Edge Function to validate status transitions, update lead.status and lead.updated_at, and optionally add note to internal_notes.
subtasks.details:
Create supabase/functions/update-lead-status/index.ts. Accepts POST with body: {lead_id, new_status, note?}. Validate: (1) user is authenticated, (2) user is agent_id or admin (reuse existing auth pattern from other functions), (3) new_status is valid enum value, (4) optional: validate transition rules if any (e.g., cannot go from closed_won back to new - decide if needed for MVP). Update: UPDATE leads SET status = $new_status, updated_at = now(), internal_notes = CASE WHEN $note THEN internal_notes || '
[' || to_char(now(), 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI') || '] ' || $note ELSE internal_notes END WHERE id = $lead_id. Return updated lead. Use _shared/ patterns for auth, validation, CORS, error handling.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Only agent_id of lead or admin can update, (2) Invalid status returns 400, (3) Status updates correctly, (4) updated_at is set to now(), (5) Note appends to internal_notes with timestamp, (6) Returns updated lead object

tasks.title: Design branding and component system (pending client approval)
tasks.description: Create Urbea brand identity (logo, color palette, typography) and design system using CODE-FIRST approach (theme.ts + component library in preview), deferring Figma to Full/Dev seat availability
tasks.details:
⚠️ GATE: Per PRD section 8 and CLAUDE.md §8, do NOT start implementation until client gives express approval.

REORIENTATION (exploration 001-branding-design-system-urbea, approved 2026-06-21): CODE-FIRST route instead of Figma-first. Figma seat is View/Starter (~6 MCP calls/month), insufficient for iteration; Figma DEFERRED until Full/Dev seat.

DESIGN DECISIONS (derived from URBEARG sibling project):
- Direction A: dual-mode tokens with SINGLE brand-core (light=management, dark=feed) → one theme.ts, never hardcoded colors
- Canonical palette = URBEARG SHIPPED: primary #1F3B2D, accent #B08968, sand #EEE5DA, ink #1A1A1A
- Dark feed derivation (WCAG AA to verify): bg #0F1A11, text #F7F3EC, accent lightened #C79A77
- Typography: Fraunces (premium serif, optical sizing) for display/titles + Inter for body/UI
- Logo: 2-3 concepts to approve direction; final artwork later

PHASES (replaces Figma-centric plan):
0. Gate: client approval
1. Identity/palette: logo concepts, canonical palette confirmation, typography selection
2. Tokens: theme.ts (light/dark), spacing/typography/radius/shadow scales
3. Base components: Button/Input/Card/Badge/BottomSheet in HTML/React preview (NOT Figma)
4. Key screens: Feed (dark), Detail (dark→light), Wizard (light), CRM (light)
5. Figma DEFERRED to seat upgrade

BRANDING GATE REMAINS ACTIVE (CLAUDE.md §8): no implementation without client approval. Doc: .taskmaster/docs/exploraciones/001-branding-design-system-urbea.md
tasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Client approved brand direction and canonical palette (GATE blocker), (2) Direction A (dual-mode tokens with single brand-core) confirmed, (3) WCAG 2.1 AA contrast verified on critical feed pairs (text on dark bg, accent on dark), (4) theme.ts (prospective) with semantic snake_case tokens for light and dark, (5) Base component library (Button/Input/Card/Badge/BottomSheet) consumes tokens and renders in preview, (6) 4 key screens mocked up with component library, (7) Feed contrasts (dark/immersive) vs management (light/clean) cohesively, (8) Figma scope deferred and reflected in plan, (9) pnpm tsc --noEmit and pnpm lint clean on theme/component code (when implemented)

subtasks.description: Present Direction A (dual-mode tokens with single URBEARG-derived brand-core), canonical palette (shipped URBEARG values), dark feed derivation, Fraunces+Inter typography, and 2-3 logo concepts. Secure explicit client approval before proceeding to implementation.

Present to client: (1) Direction A rationale - dual-mode tokens (light management, dark feed) with single brand-core from URBEARG; (2) Canonical palette - SHIPPED URBEARG values (primary #1F3B2D, accent #B08968, sand #EEE5DA, ink #1A1A1A), reconciliation note (shipped wins over spec); (3) Dark feed derivation - bg #0F1A11, text #F7F3EC, accent #C79A77 (WCAG AA to verify); (4) Typography - Fraunces (serif, optical sizing) for display, Inter for body/UI; (5) 2-3 logo direction concepts (wordmark/symbol/combination); (6) Code-first approach with Figma deferred. Document approval decision and any client modifications. Do NOT proceed to subtask 2 without explicit approval.

subtasks.description: Finalize canonical URBEARG-derived palette (light mode ~1:1) and dark feed derivation, verify critical contrast pairs against WCAG 2.1 AA, document in exploration 001.
subtasks.details: Brand-core (source): primary #1F3B2D (hover #0F1A11), accent #B08968 (hover #9A7356), sand #EEE5DA, ink #1A1A1A. Light mode tokens (management screens): color_bg=#EEE5DA, color_surface=#FFFFFF, color_text=#1A1A1A, color_text_muted=#78716C, color_border=#E7E5E4, color_primary=#1F3B2D, color_accent=#B08968. Dark mode tokens (feed): color_bg=#0F1A11, color_surface=rgba(15,26,17,0.72)+blur, color_text=#F7F3EC, color_text_muted=#C9C1B5, color_accent=#C79A77 (lightened for contrast), color_primary_on_dark=#3E6B50, color_overlay_scrim=gradient. CRITICAL: Use contrast checker tool to verify WCAG 2.1 AA (4.5:1 normal text, 3:1 large text) on: (a) #F7F3EC on #0F1A11 (feed text on bg), (b) #C79A77 on #0F1A11 (accent on dark bg), (c) #1A1A1A on #EEE5DA (light mode text). If fails, adjust lightness. Document verified values in 001-branding-design-system-urbea.md. Deliverable: palette table with verified contrast ratios.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) All token pairs documented (light and dark), (2) Critical feed pairs meet WCAG 2.1 AA (text=#F7F3EC, accent=#C79A77 on bg=#0F1A11), (3) Light mode pairs meet AA, (4) Contrast ratios documented, (5) If any fail, values adjusted and re-verified.

subtasks.title: Finalize typography system: Fraunces (display) + Inter (body/UI)
subtasks.description: Confirm Fraunces variable font (optical sizing for mobile) for display/titles and Inter for body/UI. Define type scale (display, heading h1-h4, body, caption, label) with sizes, line heights, weights.
subtasks.details: Typography decision (approved): Fraunces (serif, premium, optical sizing via opsz axis) for display/hero titles only (NOT dense UI, where serif hurts legibility on mobile); Inter (sans, workhorse) for body/paragraphs/UI. Both available via Google Fonts and loadable in RN/Expo via expo-font/@expo-google-fonts. Fraunces is variable → leverage opsz (optical size) and wght (weight) axes; potential SOFT/WONK for personality in large titles. Type scale to define: (1) Display - Fraunces opsz=72 wght=600-700, size ~32-40px, line-height 1.1-1.2, usage: hero titles (property detail, feed overlay price); (2) Heading h1-h4 - Fraunces opsz=18-24 wght=500-600, sizes 24/20/18/16px, line-height 1.3, usage: section headers; (3) Body - Inter wght=400-500, size 16/14px, line-height 1.5, usage: paragraphs, descriptions; (4) Caption - Inter wght=400, size 12px, line-height 1.4, usage: metadata, timestamps; (5) Label - Inter wght=500-600, size 14/12px, usage: buttons, form labels. Specify font file loading strategy (expo-font loadAsync or expo-google-fonts package). Document in 001 and prepare for theme.ts implementation. Deliverable: type scale table with font family, size, line-height, weight, usage per level.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify: (1) Fraunces and Inter confirmed and available via Expo, (2) Type scale covers all UI needs (display to caption), (3) Fraunces usage limited to display/titles (not dense UI), (4) Sizes are mobile-appropriate (16px+ for body, 12px min for caption), (5) Optical sizing strategy documented (opsz axis values), (6) Scale ready for theme.ts export.

subtasks.title: Accessibility audit and improvements

subtasks.title: Error handling audit and user-friendly messages
subtasks.description: Ensure all API calls have try/catch, show user-friendly errors in Spanish, and implement offline detection
subtasks.details: Error handling scope: (1) Wrap all Supabase/Edge Function calls in try/catch, (2) Create centralized error handler utility that maps Supabase error codes to user-friendly Spanish messages, (3) Implement toast/alert system for transient errors (network issues, validation), (4) Show inline errors for form validation failures, (5) Add offline detection with NetInfo - show banner when offline, disable submit buttons, queue actions if possible, (6) Handle specific scenarios: 401 unauthorized -> redirect to login, 403 forbidden -> show access denied, 404 not found -> show empty state, 500 server error -> show retry button, (7) Log errors to console with context for debugging, (8) Ensure error boundaries catch render errors and show fallback UI.
subtasks.testStrategy: Simulate network failures - app shows friendly error and doesn't crash. Invalid form submission shows inline errors. Offline mode detected and indicated to user.

subtasks.title: End-to-end testing of complete user flows
subtasks.description: Test all user flows end-to-end: admin creates agency, owner generates invitation, agent redeems code, agent publishes property, property appears in feed/map, interactions work, leads appear in CRM
subtasks.details: Execute complete flow sequence: (1) Admin login -> create agency with name/logo -> create owner user with email, (2) Owner login -> generate invitation code -> verify code appears in list, (3) New user -> enter invitation code -> complete registration -> complete onboarding with name/photo -> verify agency membership, (4) Agent publishes property -> wizard step 1 (operation/type) -> step 2 (details/location on map) -> step 3 (video upload) -> publish -> verify property status is active, (5) Switch to different agent account -> browse feed -> verify new property appears -> apply filters (operation, type, price, location) -> verify filter works, (6) Open map view -> verify property pin appears -> tap pin -> verify detail card, (7) Open property detail -> like video -> save property -> tap contact WhatsApp button -> verify lead is created, (8) Switch to original agent -> open CRM -> verify lead appears with correct property origin -> change lead status through stages, (9) Open my listings -> verify property appears -> pause property -> verify not in feed -> unpause -> verify returns to feed.

subtasks.title: Test edge cases and failure scenarios
subtasks.description: Test edge cases: network offline, slow uploads, invalid data, expired tokens, deleted properties, concurrent modifications
subtasks.details: Edge case scenarios to test: (1) Network offline - attempt login, save, publish with network disabled -> verify friendly error messages, (2) Slow video uploads - use network throttling to simulate 3G -> verify progress indicator, resumable upload works, (3) Invalid data - submit forms with: empty fields, too-long text (>500 chars), invalid email formats, negative prices, future dates -> verify validation errors, (4) Expired invitation token - attempt to redeem code after expiration -> verify clear error, (5) Expired auth token - let session expire -> attempt action -> verify redirect to login, (6) Deleted property - bookmark a property, have another account delete it, view bookmarks -> verify graceful handling (removed or grayed out), (7) Concurrent modifications - two accounts modify same lead status simultaneously -> verify no data corruption, (8) Large payload - upload maximum size video (100MB) -> verify upload completes or shows size error, (9) Deep linking - open property detail via deep link when logged out -> verify redirect flow, (10) App backgrounded during upload - minimize app during video upload -> verify upload continues or resumes correctly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Each edge case documented with steps to reproduce, expected behavior, and actual result. No crashes, no data corruption, all error messages are user-friendly Spanish text.

subtasks.title: Deploy Edge Functions to remote Supabase
subtasks.description: Deploy all Edge Functions (invitations, properties, contact-agent) to remote Supabase project with supabase functions deploy
subtasks.details: Edge Functions deployment: (1) List all Edge Functions in supabase/functions/: invitations (canje de código), properties (publicación), contact-agent (lead creation), any _shared helpers, (2) Verify each function works locally with `supabase functions serve` and test with curl/Postman, (3) Set environment variables on remote project via dashboard or CLI for any secrets (not in repo), (4) Deploy all functions: `supabase functions deploy --project-ref mvpvqmyhrrkwbnpctpuq`, (5) If deploying individually: `supabase functions deploy <function-name>`, (6) Verify deployment in Supabase dashboard -> Edge Functions -> check all functions are listed and active, (7) Test each function against remote with curl - verify responses match local behavior, (8) Check function logs in dashboard for any startup errors, (9) Document deployed function versions and any configuration notes.
subtasks.testStrategy: All Edge Functions deploy without errors. Each function responds correctly when called against production URL. Logs show no errors. Function-level tests pass against remote.

subtasks.title: Test app against production Supabase with seed data
subtasks.description: Configure app to use production Supabase, insert seed data (3-5 agencies, 10-20 properties with videos), and verify all features work
subtasks.details: Production testing setup: (1) Update app .env to point to production Supabase URL and anon key (urbea-app project ref mvpvqmyhrrkwbnpctpuq), (2) Create seed data script or manual inserts: 3-5 agencies with realistic Mexican real estate agency names, 1-2 owners per agency, 2-3 agents per agency with profile photos, 10-20 properties across agencies with: varied types (departamento, casa, terreno), varied operations (venta, renta), real Mexico City locations (CDMX, Polanco, Santa Fe, Coyoacán), realistic prices in MXN, (3) Upload 10-20 test videos to Supabase Storage (vertical format, 15-60 seconds, <50MB each), (4) Insert property_videos records with storage_path pointing to uploaded videos, (5) Run complete user flow test against production: browse feed, filter, map, detail, like, save, contact, (6) Verify all data displays correctly, videos play, map pins render, (7) Test as different user roles (admin, owner, agent), (8) Document any production-specific issues.
subtasks.testStrategy: App connects to production without errors. All seed data displays correctly. Videos play from Supabase Storage. Map renders with correct locations. Complete user flow works end-to-end on production.

subtasks.title: Build production app bundles with EAS
subtasks.description: Build production app bundles for iOS and Android using EAS Build for TestFlight and Internal App Sharing distribution
subtasks.details: EAS Build process: (1) Verify eas.json configuration with production profile pointing to production Supabase environment, (2) Update app.config.js/app.json with: correct bundleIdentifier/package name, version number (1.0.0), build number increment, production environment variables, (3) Ensure all native dependencies are compatible with EAS Build (check expo doctor), (4) For iOS: verify Apple Developer account configured, provisioning profiles for ad-hoc/App Store distribution, (5) For Android: verify keystore configured for signing, (6) Build iOS: `eas build --platform ios --profile production`, (7) Build Android: `eas build --platform android --profile production`, (8) Monitor build progress in EAS dashboard, (9) Download artifacts when complete (.ipa for iOS, .aab/.apk for Android), (10) Document build IDs, versions, and any build warnings/errors.
subtasks.testStrategy: Both iOS and Android builds complete successfully. Build artifacts download correctly. App launches without crash on simulator/emulator from production build.

subtasks.title: Internal testing with TestFlight and Internal App Sharing
subtasks.description: Distribute builds to internal testers via TestFlight (iOS) and Google Play Internal App Sharing (Android), gather feedback
subtasks.details: Internal distribution: (1) iOS TestFlight: upload .ipa to App Store Connect (via EAS Submit or Transporter), add internal testers to TestFlight group, send invitations, (2) Android Internal App Sharing: upload .aab to Google Play Console internal testing track, add tester emails, generate internal testing link, (3) Create testing instructions document for testers: how to install, test accounts/credentials, key flows to test, how to report bugs, (4) Monitor TestFlight/Play Console for crash reports and feedback, (5) Coordinate with at least 2-3 internal testers to install and run through core flows, (6) Collect feedback on: performance, UI/UX issues, bugs, missing features, (7) Triage feedback - critical bugs vs nice-to-have improvements, (8) Fix critical bugs discovered during internal testing, (9) If major issues found, iterate: fix -> rebuild -> redistribute.
subtasks.testStrategy: At least 3 internal testers successfully install and complete core user flow. Crash rate <1%. No critical bugs blocking demo. Feedback documented and triaged.

subtasks.title: Create demo checklist, monitor logs, document known issues
subtasks.description: Create demo checklist document for client validation, monitor production logs, fix critical bugs, and document known issues and post-demo improvements
subtasks.details: Final polish and documentation: (1) Create demo checklist document (docs/DEMO-CHECKLIST.md) with: prerequisite setup (test accounts, seed data verification), step-by-step demo script for client validation matching PRD-MVP-demo.md acceptance criteria, known limitations to mention during demo, (2) Set up log monitoring: check Supabase dashboard logs, Edge Function logs for errors, enable Sentry if configured, (3) Monitor production for 24-48 hours before demo - identify and fix any critical bugs, (4) Document known issues in docs/KNOWN-ISSUES.md: categorize as critical/medium/low, note workarounds if any, (5) Create post-demo improvements backlog: features deferred from MVP, performance optimizations identified, UX improvements from tester feedback, technical debt to address, (6) Prepare rollback plan: how to revert to previous state if demo catastrophically fails, (7) Final smoke test: run through complete demo checklist to verify everything works, (8) Update wiki/estado/estado-actual.md with final demo-ready state.
subtasks.testStrategy: Demo checklist document complete and reviewed. Logs show no critical errors in 24h. Known issues documented with severity. Post-demo backlog prioritized. Complete demo dry-run successful.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down polish into: (1) code review and refactoring pass, (2) performance optimization for video and images, (3) accessibility audit, (4) error handling audit, (5) end-to-end testing of complete user flows, (6) test edge cases and failure scenarios, (7) apply migration 0011 to remote Supabase, (8) deploy Edge Functions to remote, (9) test app against production with seed data, (10) build production bundles with EAS, (11) internal testing with TestFlight/Internal App Sharing, (12) create demo checklist and document known issues
